# Confidential mode
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/apps/confidential
The second privacy mode — hidden amounts between public addresses. Testnet preview.

Confidential Tokens are the **complementary** Stellar privacy tool: they hide *amounts and
balances* while keeping sender and recipient addresses public — the inverse trade-off of the
shielded pool. Useful when the relationship is known (payroll, invoices between named parties)
but the numbers are nobody's business.

## Status: testnet-only, unaudited preview

This mode is original ZK Fighter work — Noir circuits proved with UltraHonk (`bb.js`) in the
browser, balances as homomorphic commitments on the Grumpkin curve, and a Soroban SEP-41
wrapper contract (built on OpenZeppelin's confidential-token preview). It is deliberately
scoped to **testnet** and clearly labeled in every surface.

## Operations

| Operation | What it does |
|---|---|
| Register | Creates your confidential account on the token |
| Deposit | Wraps public USDC into a hidden balance |
| Merge | Consolidates pending amounts into your spendable balance |
| Transfer | Sends a hidden amount to another registered address |
| Withdraw | Unwraps back to public USDC |

Every operation generates its proof on your device, like the shielded pool.

## Where to find it

The **Confidential** screen on web and extension, and the mobile Confidential flow. Amounts are
hidden; the participating addresses are public — the screen says exactly that.

---

# Browser extension
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/apps/extension
The quick companion — glance, QuickShield, receive.

The extension is the everyday companion: a fast popup for balances and quick actions, with a
side panel for heavier flows. Same seed, same receive code, same pool as web and mobile.

## Install (developer build)

```bash
pnpm extension:build
# then chrome://extensions → Developer mode → Load unpacked
# → apps/extension/.output/chrome-mv3
```

## What the popup does

- **Instant balances** — cached balances render immediately on open and refresh in the
  background (with an honest syncing indicator, never fabricated zeros)
- **QuickShield** — shield XLM or USDC in a couple of taps; first-time access setup runs
  automatically in the same flow
- **Receive** — `zkf1…` code and public address with QR
- **Activity, settings, disclosure, discovery** — via bottom tabs and the More menu

Heavy proving runs in an offscreen document, so the popup never freezes — see
[Extension internals](/docs/architecture/extension).

## The boundary with dApps

Sites can detect that a Stellar wallet is present, but requests for your public key or a
signature are **refused by design**. ZK Fighter is a privacy wallet, not a general signer.

---

# Mobile app
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/apps/mobile
The same wallet, thumb-first — Capacitor on Android and iOS.

The mobile app is a native Capacitor shell around the same core engine: real vault, real
proving, real transactions — thumb-first. Swipe between the shielded and public balance cards,
run flows in bottom sheets, and prove in-sheet.

## Run it

```bash
pnpm mobile:dev        # browser preview → http://127.0.0.1:4183
pnpm mobile:android    # native Android project (Android Studio)
pnpm mobile:ios        # native iOS project (Xcode)
```

### Testing from a real phone browser

A phone can't use `http://<lan-ip>` (no secure context → no Web Crypto) and rejects self-signed
HTTPS. The repo ships a tunnel workflow that solves both:

```bash
pnpm --filter @zk-fighter/mobile dev:phone   # dev server with same-origin /zkf-* API proxies
pnpm --filter @zk-fighter/mobile tunnel      # prints a trusted https://….trycloudflare.com URL
```

Open the printed URL on the phone — trusted HTTPS, no mixed content, full functionality.

## Honest status

- Shielded balances load via an explicit **Sync** (keeps background storage pressure off mobile
  browsers)
- Proof support on physical devices is gated behind an on-device runtime check — the app
  verifies WebCrypto, workers, and WASM before claiming it
- Bridge on mobile starts as a hand-off to the web app

---

# Web app
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/apps/web
The full wallet in the browser.

The web app is the complete ZK Fighter experience: create/import a wallet, fund (testnet),
shield, send privately, unshield, bridge, disclosure, discovery, confidential mode, activity,
and settings.

```bash
pnpm web:dev   # → http://127.0.0.1:5173
```

## Layout

A sidebar navigates the full feature set; the home screen pairs the **shielded balance card**
(private) with the **public balance card** (boundary-labeled), separated by a "cross the
boundary" action strip — shield, unshield, bridge.

## Things worth knowing

- **Balances**: public balances load from Horizon; shielded balances come from scanning pool
  notes — use **Sync balance** after restoring a wallet or switching devices.
- **USDC receiving** (the trustline) is established automatically the first time you fund,
  shield, or bridge — there is no manual setup step.
- **Activity** spans both worlds: shielded entries hide their detail; public boundary legs are
  labeled and link to the explorer.
- **Lock wallet** clears the unlocked vault from memory; your password unlocks it again. The
  seed phrase remains the only recovery path.

---

# Extension internals
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/architecture/extension
How heavy proving fits inside a Manifest V3 extension.

MV3 popups are short-lived and service workers can't run the multi-megabyte WASM prover
comfortably. The extension splits responsibilities:

<Mermaid
  chart={`flowchart LR
    popup["Popup / side panel UI"] --> bg["Background service worker"]
    bg --> off["Offscreen document<br/>WASM prover + pool runtime"]
    off --> rpc[("Stellar RPC / Horizon")]
    dapp["dApp page"] -.->|"public-key access & signing<br/>fail closed by design"| bg`}
/>

- **Popup** — the fast glance surface: balances (served instantly from cache, refreshed in the
  background), QuickShield, receive, activity.
- **Side panel** — where heavier flows (send, unshield, bridge) get room to breathe.
- **Offscreen document** — hosts the Nethermind WASM prover and pool runtime; the background
  worker proxies typed messages to it.

## The dApp boundary fails closed

ZK Fighter answers Freighter-style detection pings so sites know a Stellar wallet exists — but
**external public-key access and transaction signing are refused by design**. It is a privacy
wallet, not a general dApp signer. A site can never enumerate your address or ask you to sign.

## Verification harnesses

The repo ships CDP-driven runtime checks that launch a real Chrome, load the built extension,
and drive real flows — including a fully funded shield on testnet:

```bash
pnpm extension:runtime      # popup renders, routes work, prover initializes
pnpm extension:quickshield  # real funded XLM shield end-to-end
```

---

# Overview
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/architecture/overview
One core engine, three thin surfaces, two supporting services.

Every app is a thin presentation layer over one shared package, `@zk-fighter/core`, which owns
identity, vault, proving, networks, and transactions. If a flow works on web it works on the
extension and mobile, because they run the same code.

<Mermaid
  chart={`flowchart LR
    subgraph Surfaces
      web["apps/web"]
      ext["apps/extension"]
      mob["apps/mobile"]
    end
    subgraph Shared
      core["packages/core<br/>identity · vault · proofs · tx"]
      ui["packages/ui<br/>design system"]
    end
    subgraph Services
      boot["apps/bootnode<br/>warmed event index"]
      fund["apps/funding-api<br/>testnet faucet"]
    end
    web --> core
    ext --> core
    mob --> core
    web --> ui
    ext --> ui
    mob --> ui
    core -->|"getEvents (warmed)"| boot
    core -->|"demo funding"| fund
    core --> stellar[("Stellar / Soroban<br/>privacy pool contracts")]
    core --> cctp["Circle CCTP<br/>USDC bridge"]`}
/>

## The monorepo

| Path | What it is |
|---|---|
| `apps/web` | React wallet app — the primary surface |
| `apps/extension` | WXT MV3 browser extension (popup + side panel, offscreen prover) |
| `apps/mobile` | Capacitor app (Android/iOS) reusing the same core |
| `apps/landing` | Product site |
| `apps/bootnode` | Warmed pool-event indexer + narrow RPC (Postgres) |
| `apps/funding-api` | Hosted testnet faucet (XLM + USDC, budgets and cooldowns) |
| `packages/core` | All wallet, network, proof, and transaction logic |
| `packages/ui` | Shared design system |
| `circuits/`, `contracts/` | Noir circuits + Soroban contract for Confidential Token mode |

## Networks are configuration

Testnet ↔ mainnet is a config toggle, not a code change: RPC/Horizon endpoints, pool contract
IDs, asset contracts, and bridge routes all come from one network config. The UI always shows
which network you're on.

## Two privacy modes, two proving stacks

| | Shielded Pools (flagship) | Confidential Tokens (preview) |
|---|---|---|
| Hides | balances, amounts, counterparties | amounts and balances only |
| Proving | Circom / Groth16, Rust→WASM | Noir + UltraHonk (bb.js) |
| Networks | testnet + mainnet | testnet only |

Read more in [Proving](/docs/architecture/proving) and
[Confidential mode](/docs/apps/confidential).

---

# Proving on-device
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/architecture/proving
The Groth16 pipeline that runs in your browser — and the second Noir stack.

## Shielded pools: Circom / Groth16

The flagship path reuses Nethermind's `stellar-private-payments` engine (credited openly):

- **Circuit:** a 2-in/2-out "policy transaction" — every operation (deposit, transfer,
  withdrawal) is the same circuit with different public amounts. Change returns as a hidden
  note.
- **Prover:** Rust compiled to WebAssembly, running in the browser (in a worker on web/mobile,
  in an offscreen document in the extension). A proof takes ~6 seconds on a laptop.
- **Verifier:** the Soroban pool contract verifies the Groth16 proof on-chain over BN254 with
  Poseidon2 hashing — the primitives Stellar added natively in Protocols 25/26.
- **Binding:** the recipient and amount of a withdrawal are hashed into the proof
  (`ext_data_hash`), so nobody — not even a submitting service — can redirect funds.

## Confidential tokens: Noir / UltraHonk

The second mode (testnet-only preview) is original ZK Fighter work: Noir circuits
(`circuits/`) proved with Barretenberg's UltraHonk via `bb.js`, verified on-chain by a Soroban
verifier, with balances as homomorphic commitments on the Grumpkin curve
(`contracts/confidential-token`). Operations: register, deposit, merge, transfer, withdraw.

## Why proofs never leave your device

Both stacks generate proofs locally. A server-side prover would see your amounts and
counterparties — the exact thing the product exists to protect. The trade-off is honest: the
first load pulls multi-megabyte WASM artifacts, and proving costs a few seconds of CPU. The
wallet shows real progress for both.

---

# Services
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/architecture/services
The bootnode event index and the hosted testnet faucet.

The wallet works against public Stellar infrastructure alone. Two small self-hosted services
make it resilient and friction-free:

## Bootnode — warmed event index

Stellar's public RPC only retains recent events. Shielded wallets need the pool's **full**
commitment history to find their notes — so the bootnode continuously indexes pool events into
Postgres from the pool's deployment ledger, then serves them through a narrow RPC.

- **Allowlisted**: only `getEvents` and `getLatestLedger`, only for ZK Fighter pool contracts —
  it cannot be used as a general RPC proxy.
- **Read-only**: it holds no keys and signs nothing.
- **Transparent**: clients route pool reads to it when configured (`VITE_ZKF_*_BOOTNODE_URL`)
  and fall back to public RPC otherwise.

Default ports: testnet `:8788`, mainnet `:8789`. `GET /health` reports indexing progress.

## Funding API — hosted testnet faucet

Removes the faucet hunt on testnet: one request delivers XLM and USDC to a new wallet, setting
up the USDC trustline automatically. Budgeted, cooldown-limited, backed by a capped hot wallet —
and it **refuses mainnet** by design.

Default port `:8787`; endpoints `GET /v1/funding/status` and `POST /v1/funding/request`.

## Deployment posture

Every surface is an env-driven build (`PORT`, `VITE_ZKF_*`); nothing assumes a specific host.
See `docs/deploy/production-surfaces.md` in the repo for the full variable reference.

---

# Evidence & verification
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/evidence
Every claim is backed by accepted on-chain transactions you can check yourself.

ZK Fighter's rule is **no fakes**: no mocked balances, no fabricated hashes, no claimed flows
without accepted transactions. The full evidence table — 40+ real transaction hashes across
Stellar testnet, Stellar mainnet, and four EVM testnets — lives in the repository README, with
the deeper audit trail in the docs folder:

- [README evidence table](https://github.com/Blockchain-Oracle/zk-fighter#evidence)
- [Verified facts record](https://github.com/Blockchain-Oracle/zk-fighter/blob/main/docs/VERIFIED-FACTS.md)

Spot-check any Stellar hash on [stellar.expert](https://stellar.expert/explorer/testnet), and
EVM hashes on the respective chain explorer.

## What's proven, at a glance

- **Testnet:** the full loop — fund, shield, shielded transfer, unshield — for USDC and XLM,
  from the web app, the extension (via its CDP harness), and the mobile surface
- **Mainnet:** QuickShield (XLM + USDC), shielded transfers, and unshields with accepted hashes
- **Bridge:** complete bridge-to-shield evidence from Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, and OP Sepolia
- **Load-bearing ZK:** a tampered proof is rejected on-chain (the wallet ships a demo of this)

## What's explicitly NOT claimed

- Mainnet **bridge-to-shield** (no accepted hashes yet)
- Atomic bridge-and-shield (deferred until a custom adapter passes real tests)
- Any audit. This is early, unaudited software — testnet is the recommended surface.

## Run the verification yourself

```bash
pnpm test                   # 265 tests
pnpm extension:runtime      # extension runtime smoke in a real Chrome
pnpm extension:quickshield  # real funded shield, end to end, on testnet
```

---

# Glossary
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/glossary
Every term in plain English — assume smart, not cryptographer.

## Privacy concepts

- **Zero-knowledge proof (ZK)** — proving a statement is true without revealing the underlying
  data. Like proving you're over 21 without showing your birthday. Here: proving a payment is
  valid without revealing the amount or who's involved.
- **Shielded transfer** — Stellar's own term for a payment whose sensitive details are hidden
  with cryptography. The precise word for what ZK Fighter does — narrower and more honest than
  "fully private."
- **Privacy pool** — a shared on-chain pot many people deposit into and withdraw from. Inside,
  payments are hidden because an observer can't tell which deposit connects to which withdrawal.
  The edges (deposits/withdrawals) stay visible.
- **Shield / deposit** — moving money *into* the pool. Public.
- **Unshield / withdraw** — moving money *out* of the pool. Public.
- **Commitment** — a sealed on-chain receipt proving a note exists without revealing amount or
  owner.
- **Nullifier** — a one-time "spent" stamp that prevents double-spending without being traceable
  to the deposit.
- **Merkle tree** — bundles many records into one fingerprint so you can prove membership
  ("my note is in the pool") without revealing the rest.
- **ASP / association set** — an on-chain membership list deciding which deposits may
  participate, so the pool can screen out flagged funds. How privacy and compliance coexist.
- **Selective disclosure / view evidence** — read-only proof of your own note for an auditor,
  with zero spend authority.
- **Confidential Token** — the complementary Stellar privacy tool: hides *amounts and balances*
  but keeps addresses public (the inverse trade-off of a privacy pool). ZK Fighter ships it as a
  second, testnet-only mode.

## Keys and recovery

- **Seed phrase** — 12 ordinary words; the master backup. Anyone holding it controls the funds.
  No reset, no backdoor. ZK Fighter's only recovery path.
- **Vault password** — unlocks the encrypted wallet *on this device*. Different from the seed
  phrase; it cannot recover funds.
- **Private receive code (`zkf1…`)** — the shareable code that lets others pay your shielded
  balance. Contains no spending power. See [receive codes](/docs/how-it-works/receive-codes).
- **Passkey** — optional device-biometric unlock (WebAuthn/PRF) layered over the seed-backed
  vault. Never a recovery method on its own.

## Plumbing

- **Soroban** — Stellar's smart-contract platform, where the pool and verifier contracts run.
- **SAC (Stellar Asset Contract)** — the standard wrapper letting assets like USDC be used in
  Soroban contracts.
- **Circle CCTP** — Circle's burn-and-mint bridge delivering *native* USDC across chains.
- **Groth16 / BN254 / Poseidon** — the proof system, elliptic curve, and hash function used by
  the shielded pool; Stellar Protocols 25/26 added native support that makes on-chain
  verification affordable.
- **Bootnode** — ZK Fighter's warmed event index: Stellar's public RPC only retains recent
  events, so this service remembers pool history and serves it through a narrow, allowlisted
  RPC.

---

# Bridging USDC in
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/how-it-works/bridge
Real Circle USDC from Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, or Optimism — then shield it.

ZK Fighter uses **Circle's CCTP** (Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol) to bring native USDC onto
Stellar. CCTP burns USDC on the source chain and mints genuine USDC on Stellar — no wrapped
IOU. Supported sources: **Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Optimism** (Sepolia testnets are the
demo surface).

<Mermaid
  chart={`sequenceDiagram
    participant E as EVM chain (Base / Arbitrum / OP / Ethereum)
    participant I as Circle Iris attestation
    participant X as Stellar
    E->>E: USDC approve + depositForBurn
    E-->>I: burn message
    I-->>X: signed attestation
    X->>X: receiveMessage → USDC mint (public arrival)
    X->>X: separate shield/deposit into the privacy pool`}
/>

## The burn is signed by your own wallet

The EVM-side burn is signed with an **EVM key derived from your same seed phrase** — no
MetaMask, no WalletConnect, no separate wallet to manage.

## Two honest steps, not one fake atomic

Circle's contract mints USDC to the recipient — it does not call the privacy pool. So the flow
is deliberately two labeled steps:

1. **Bridge arrival (public):** burn → Circle attestation (this can take minutes; the UI shows
   real progress) → Stellar mint. Every leg has a real transaction hash.
2. **Shield (public boundary):** a separate deposit moves the arrived USDC into the pool.

An atomic bridge-and-shield would require a custom adapter contract; it stays out of the product
until it exists and passes real tests. We do not fake it.

---

# Selective disclosure
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/how-it-works/disclosure
Prove a specific note to an auditor — read-only, on your terms.

Privacy products live or die on compliance. ZK Fighter's answer is **user-held selective
disclosure**: you can prove facts about your own shielded funds to someone you choose, without
giving them any power over the funds.

## How it works

The Disclosure screen produces a **read-only proof of a specific note** — its existence and your
ownership — that a counterparty or auditor can verify. Two powers stay deliberately separated:

- **Spend authority** — never leaves your wallet
- **View evidence** — the disclosure receipt, which you hand over explicitly

Proving runs on your device; nothing is uploaded. The receipt covers exactly the note you chose —
not your balance, not your other notes, not your history.

## Verifying a receipt

The same screen verifies receipts you receive from others, so both sides of an audit can run it
without trusting a server.

## Why this design

The alternative — a custodial relayer that logs identities for compliance — puts a middleman in
every payment who can see everything. ZK Fighter's association-set gate (screening at the pool
door) plus user-held disclosure (proof on demand) achieves compliance without anyone holding a
master view of your activity.

---

# Private receive codes
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/how-it-works/receive-codes
The zkf1… code that lets anyone pay your shielded balance without knowing your address.

To be paid privately, you share a **private receive code** — a `zkf1…` string (Bech32m encoding,
human-readable prefix `zkf`) shown as text and QR in every surface's Receive screen.

## What's inside

The code packs two public keys derived from your seed:

- your **note public key** — ownership of the notes a sender creates for you
- your **encryption public key** — lets the sender encrypt the note details so only you can
  discover them on-chain

It contains **no spending power and no link to your Stellar address**. Sharing it is safe; it
only lets people pay you privately.

## Discovery

Your wallet finds incoming notes by scanning the pool's encrypted outputs with your keys —
locally. Nobody else can tell which notes are yours.

## Optional: make your code discoverable

If you want people to find your code from your public Stellar address, you can opt in to
publishing the link on-chain ("Make my code discoverable" in the Receive screen). This is a
**public, deliberate act** — it never exposes your seed or any power to spend, only the mapping
from address to receive code. Skip it and share the code out-of-band for maximum privacy.

## Recovery

Codes are deterministic: restoring your wallet from the 12-word phrase regenerates the same
receive code on any device.

---

# Shielded transfers
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/how-it-works/shielded-transfers
Notes, nullifiers, and on-device proofs — the full lifecycle of private money.

"Shielded" is Stellar's own word for a payment whose sensitive details are hidden with
cryptography. Here is the whole lifecycle, with the public boundaries marked:

<Mermaid
  chart={`sequenceDiagram
    participant S as Sender wallet
    participant P as Soroban privacy pool
    participant R as Recipient wallet
    rect rgba(229,180,92,0.07)
        Note over S,P: SHIELD — public boundary
        S->>P: deposit XLM/USDC + note commitment (public tx)
    end
    rect rgba(94,124,250,0.08)
        Note over S,R: SHIELDED TRANSFER — inside the pool
        S->>S: generate Groth16 proof on-device (~6s)
        S->>P: proof + nullifiers + new commitments
        R->>P: discover notes via encrypted outputs
    end
    rect rgba(229,180,92,0.07)
        Note over R,P: UNSHIELD — public boundary
        R->>P: withdrawal proof
        P->>R: XLM/USDC to a public address (public tx)
    end`}
/>

## The moving parts

- **Commitment** — a sealed, tamper-proof receipt posted on-chain when money enters the pool.
  It proves a note exists without revealing its amount or owner: a locked box everyone can see
  but nobody can open.
- **Nullifier** — a unique one-time "spent" stamp published when you spend a note. It prevents
  double-spending but cannot be traced back to the original deposit.
- **Merkle tree** — all commitments are bundled into one short fingerprint, so a proof can show
  "my note is in the pool" without revealing which one.
- **The proof** — a Groth16 zero-knowledge proof over the BN254 curve, generated by a Rust→WASM
  prover **in your browser** (recorded at roughly six seconds on a laptop), then verified by the
  pool contract on-chain. Stellar's Protocol 25/26 upgrades added the native BN254 and Poseidon
  primitives that make on-chain verification affordable.

## First-time setup: shield access

Before your first shield, the wallet registers a membership leaf with the on-chain
**association-set (ASP)** contract. This is the compliance layer — the pool can include
known-good participants and exclude flagged funds. The wallet handles it in one continuous run:
setup transaction → wait for on-chain indexing → deposit, with live progress.

## Change stays private

Shielded transfers use a 2-in/2-out note design: when you spend, change comes back to you as a
**new hidden note**. There is no visible change output to correlate.

## Credit where due

The shielded-pool engine — circuits, browser prover, and pool contracts — is Nethermind's
open-source `stellar-private-payments` reference implementation (Privacy Pools on Stellar).
ZK Fighter builds the wallet product on top: the multi-surface UX, access orchestration,
receive codes, disclosure, bridge-to-shield, and services.

---

# What is ZK Fighter?
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs
A self-custody wallet for shielded XLM and USDC payments on Stellar.

ZK Fighter is a **privacy-by-default, self-custody wallet** for the Stellar network. Normally
every Stellar transaction is visible to anyone who looks. ZK Fighter lets you move **XLM and
USDC** so that the sensitive details — amounts, balances, counterparties — stay private, using
real cryptography rather than trust in a middleman.

The proofs are **zero-knowledge proofs**, generated **on your own device** in about six seconds,
and verified on-chain by Soroban smart contracts. No server ever sees your payment.

## The honest one-liner

> Public in. Private through. Your call out.

Stellar is a public blockchain — you cannot make everything disappear, and we never claim to.
What you get is **shielded transfers**: the link between money entering and leaving the pool is
broken, and payments *inside* the pool are private. Three moments are public, and the wallet
names them every time:

- **Shield in (deposit)** — visible on-chain
- **Unshield out (withdraw)** — visible on-chain
- **Bridge arrivals** — USDC arriving from another chain is visible on-chain

Everything between those boundaries is a shielded transfer.

## What it is not

- Not "anonymous", "fully private", or "untraceable" — see the [privacy model](/docs/privacy-model)
- Not a mixer — first-time shielding registers with an association-set (ASP) contract so the
  pool can screen out flagged funds
- Not a general-purpose public wallet — ZK Fighter builds the privacy layer only
- Not audited — this is early software; don't use real funds yet

## One wallet, three surfaces

The [web app](/docs/apps/web), [browser extension](/docs/apps/extension), and
[mobile app](/docs/apps/mobile) share one shielded pool and one core engine. Every feature is
consistent — only the shape changes.

## Compliance, on your terms

[Selective disclosure](/docs/how-it-works/disclosure) produces a **read-only proof of a specific
note** for an auditor or counterparty — without handing over keys or spend authority. You decide
what's seen.

---

# Privacy model
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/privacy-model
Exactly what is hidden, what is public, and what we refuse to claim.

## The three boundaries

Stellar's own documentation says it plainly: Stellar is a public blockchain — every transaction
is recorded on-chain and visible to anyone. Privacy exists inside specific tools you opt into.
ZK Fighter is built around that honesty:

| Step | Visibility | What an observer sees |
|---|---|---|
| **1 · Shield in** | ○ Public | Your address deposited X into the pool contract |
| **2 · Send privately** | ⛉ Shielded | A valid proof was accepted — no amount, balance, or counterparty |
| **3 · Unshield out** | ○ Public | An address withdrew Y from the pool |

Bridge arrivals (USDC landing from another chain via Circle CCTP) are also public — the wallet
says so in the flow.

## How the hiding actually works

Inside the pool, your money exists as **notes** — sealed commitments on-chain that prove a coin
exists without revealing its amount or owner. Spending publishes a **nullifier**, a one-time
"spent" stamp that prevents double-spends but cannot be traced back to the deposit. A
**zero-knowledge proof**, generated on your device, convinces the contract the transfer is valid
without revealing anything else. See [shielded transfers](/docs/how-it-works/shielded-transfers).

## Compliance without a custodian

- **Association set (ASP):** first-time shielding registers a membership leaf with an on-chain
  association-set contract, so the pool can include known-good funds and screen out flagged
  ones. Privacy and compliance coexist — this is not a mixer.
- **Selective disclosure:** you can produce a [read-only proof of a specific note](/docs/how-it-works/disclosure)
  for an auditor — no keys, no spend authority, nothing uploaded.

## What we refuse to claim

- **Not "anonymous", "fully private", or "untraceable."** Boundaries are public, and correlation
  is real: matching deposit and withdrawal **amounts**, close **timing**, and a small pool
  (**anonymity set**) can all weaken privacy. Sending common amounts and waiting after shielding
  improves it.
- **Today, pool transactions are signed by your own Stellar account.** An observer can link your
  account to pool activity at the envelope level (though never the amounts or counterparties of
  shielded transfers). A relayer that submits on your behalf is designed and planned — until it
  ships, we say this plainly instead of overclaiming.
- **Unaudited.** The shielded-pool engine (Nethermind's reference implementation) and this wallet
  are not security-audited. Testnet is the recommended surface; do not use real funds.

---

# Quickstart
URL: https://docs.zkfighter.dev/docs/quickstart
Run the wallet, fund it on testnet, and make your first shielded transfer.

## Prerequisites

- **Node 22+** and **pnpm 10** (`corepack enable`)
- The repo: `git clone https://github.com/Blockchain-Oracle/zk-fighter`

```bash
pnpm install
pnpm build     # builds every workspace
pnpm test      # full test suite
```

## Run the web wallet

```bash
pnpm web:dev   # → http://127.0.0.1:5173
```

The wallet works against public Stellar RPC out of the box. Two optional local services improve
the experience — the hosted testnet faucet and the warmed event index:

```bash
ZKF_TESTNET_FUNDER_SECRET=SA... pnpm funding-api:dev   # faucet, :8787
pnpm bootnode:dev                                      # event index, :8788
```

## Five minutes to a shielded transfer

<Steps>
<Step>
**Create a wallet.** 12-word recovery phrase → vault password. The phrase is the only recovery
path — there is no reset and no support backdoor.
</Step>
<Step>
**Add testnet funds.** One tap delivers XLM and USDC; USDC receiving (the trustline) is set up
automatically.
</Step>
<Step>
**Shield.** Pick an amount. The wallet checks your balance, prepares shield access
(a one-time on-chain setup, first shield only), syncs the pool, generates the proof on your
device, and submits — all in one run. The deposit is labeled a **public boundary**.
</Step>
<Step>
**Send privately.** Paste the recipient's `zkf1…` private receive code, choose an amount, and
generate the proof (~6s on-device). Amount and counterparty stay inside the pool.
</Step>
<Step>
**Unshield when you choose.** Withdraw to any public address — the wallet tells you exactly
what becomes visible before you confirm.
</Step>
</Steps>

## Other surfaces

```bash
pnpm extension:build   # load apps/extension/.output/chrome-mv3 as an
                       # unpacked extension in chrome://extensions
pnpm mobile:dev        # mobile surface in the browser, :4183
```

See the [app guides](/docs/apps/web) for each surface in detail.
